_comment: REGIME 4: CLOSED CYLINDER — Fill measured values from engine pressure-trace / optical / dyno data

provenance.source: Wang, X.; Zhao, H. "Modelling Study of Cycle-To-Cycle Variations (CCV) in Spark Ignition (SI)-Controlled Auto-Ignition (CAI) Hybrid Combustion Engine by Using Reynolds-Averaged Navier–Stokes (RANS) and Large Eddy Simulation (LES)" Energies 2022, 15, 4478.

operating_bounds.notes: Single-cylinder gasoline SI–CAI hybrid combustion point. Mean IMEP=5.43 bar (100 cycles). Stoichiometric equivalence ratio=1. Spark timing = -49 °CA (BTDC). Residual gas fraction after IVC reported as 25% (calculated). Intake P/T values taken from Table 3 (simulation boundary conditions matching experiment as reported).

metrics.notes: Experimental mean peak pressure PP = 42.11 bar; σ = 11.03 bar over 100 cycles (Figure 4).
CA10 defined by 10% fuel mass fraction burned (cumulative heat release). Experimental mean = -4.33 °CA; σ = 2.58 °CA (Figure 6).
CA50 defined by 50% fuel mass fraction burned. Experimental mean = 11.15 °CA; σ = 4.15 °CA (Figure 6).
CA90 defined by 90% fuel mass fraction burned. Experimental mean = 23.26 °CA; σ = 12.34 °CA (Figure 6).

No cumulative apparent heat release integral is tabulated in the paper. Value stored here is *chemical heat input per cycle* = fueling_rate * LHV. Fueling rate = 22.4 mg/cycle (Table 2). LHV assumed 43.1 MJ/kg for gasoline (external reference); computed Qin ≈ 0.965 kJ/cycle. Treat as consistency check only (not aHR).
Experimental mean IMEP = 5.43 bar; σ = 0.64 bar over 100 cycles (Figure 3).

dataset.description: Closed-cylinder validation target extracted from Wang & Zhao (Energies 2022, 15, 4478) for a single-cylinder gasoline SI–CAI hybrid combustion operating point (1500 rpm, IMEP 5.43 bar). Provides mean and cycle-to-cycle variability for peak pressure and CA10/50/90 based on 100-cycle pressure data.
dataset.fuel: Gasoline (93 RON), port fuel injection (paper test fuel).

solver_config.notes: Experimental CA10/50/90 are based on cumulative heat release (per paper). Peak pressure and IMEP are derived directly from measured cylinder pressure. Heat_release_kJ_measured is stored as chemical heat input proxy (see dataset.metrics notes).
